questions.q: A copper wire of length 10 m is connected in series with a resistance of 100 Ω. What is the effective resistance of the circuit?
A cell of e.m.f. 2 V and internal resistance 1 Ω is connected to a resistance of 9 Ω. Find the current drawn from the cell.
A 20 V battery of emf is connected across a potential difference of 22 V. What is the work done in 10 seconds?
A 100 Ω resistor and a 200 Ω resistor are connected in series to a 5 V battery. What is the potential difference across the 200 Ω resistor?
A galvanometer of resistance 10 Ω shows a deflection of 20 μA for a current of 0.1 A. What is the current sensitivity of the galvanometer?
A 25 Ω resistor and a 50 Ω resistor are connected in parallel to a 10 V battery. What is the current flowing through the 25 Ω resistor?
A wire of resistance 2 Ω is stretched to twice its original length. What is the new resistance?
A galvanometer of resistance 15 Ω has a sensitivity of 20 μA/mA. What is the current required to produce a deflection of 50 μA?
A 100 Ω resistor and a 200 Ω resistor are connected in parallel to a 5 V battery. What is the total current drawn from the battery?
A cell of e.m.f. 3 V and internal resistance 0.5 Ω is connected to a resistance of 10 Ω. Find the potential difference across the cell.
